methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
QuantumDiamonds is a seed-stage quantum computing company founded by Kevin Berghoff and Fleming Bruckmaier. The target operates within a fragmented sector comprising 3 public peers (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private competitors, including stage-advanced peers IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. The quantum computing sector contains only 7 same-sector peers total, with zero companies meeting tight stage-match or adjacent-stage filters, and zero within a comparable valuation tier. Conventional triangulation therefore requires a minimum of 3 datapoints within a defensible peer cohort and cannot be satisfied without introducing material comparability distortion. A broad all-sector median would obscure stage-specific and business-model differences critical to seed-round pricing.

The analysis cannot support a specific valuation range without quantitative triangulation. Key uncertainties include: the absence of stage-comparable public or private benchmarks; the wide dispersion in development maturity and go-to-market readiness across the 11-company comp universe; and the early-stage nature of the target, which typically exhibits lower correlation with later-stage peer valuations. QuantumDiamonds' €7M seed raise and €152M facility commitment represent material data points but require contextual comparison to funding patterns and implied valuations among directly comparable quantum-computing seed-stage entrants—data not present in the available set. Further diligence on stage-specific financing terms and technical differentiation is necessary before valuation triangulation.
